conjecture_title: Informal question: validity of Conjecture 1.2 for trees and planar graphs
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. III. Excluding a fat tree
summary: Conjecture 1.2 of Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu — that fat-$H$-minor-free graphs are quasi-isometric to $H$-minor-free graphs — was disproved in general by Davies, Hickingbotham, Illingworth, and McCarty with a counterexample $H$ containing a clique of size 15. The source paper (arXiv:2509.09035) proves a weaker result for trees: fat-tree-minor-free graphs admit quasi-isometries to graphs of bounded line-width, but the full conjecture for $H$ a tree or planar graph remains open. No follow-up resolving the tree or planar special case was found in the indexed literature as of May 2026.
search_queries: Georgakopoulos Papasoglu conjecture minor tree planar graph asymptotic structure
Nguyen Scott Seymour "fat tree" "line-width" "Conjecture 1.2" tree planar 2025
Georgakopoulos Papasoglu "graph minors metric spaces" conjecture fat minor tree quasi-isometric 2025 2026
notes: The question is raised informally in arXiv:2509.09035 (September 2025). The paper's own main result handles trees but with a strictly weaker conclusion (quasi-isometry to bounded line-width rather than to H-minor-free), so Conjecture 1.2 for H a tree or H planar remains open. A related April 2026 paper (arXiv:2604.11318, Bonnet–Le–Pilipczuk–Pilipczuk) on coarse balanced separators in fat-minor-free graphs was found but its abstract does not address this specific conjecture.
